assessor_1_raw: Clinical deterioration prediction: the design correctly preserves irregular sampling for the sequence model while using aggregated features for the baseline. Temporal split with admission-level assignment is appropriate. Pre-registered minimum delta (0.05 AUROC) is a good clinical power estimate. Verdict: defense_wins.
assessor_2_raw: Strong clinical prediction study. Composite outcome is clinically justified. ECE and subgroup AUROC are appropriate clinical deployment metrics. Documentation frequency robustness check is an important confound control. DeLong's test is the right statistical test for paired AUROC comparison. Verdict: defense_wins.
assessor_3_raw: Well-designed clinical deterioration study. Palliative patient exclusion is clinically appropriate. The 24-hour observation window is the stated scope. The design handles within-patient leakage at the admission level. No methodological flaws. Verdict: defense_wins.
